summary: PR #2565 replaced the placeholder comment for fn_8003F294 in src/melee/pl/plbonuslib.c with a C implementation and a small inline wrapper around pl_800386D8. The PR body claims require-protos, targeted object build, full ninja build, and local objdiff verification for fn_8003F294 at 680 bytes and 100.0%; the bot report corroborates one new 680-byte match in main/melee/pl/plbonuslib.

key_files.role: Only changed source file; adds the matched body for fn_8003F294 and helper inline plBonusLib_8003F294_inline.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: replaces placeholder `/// fn_8003F294` with inline helper and fn_8003F294 implementation

what_changed.text: Added `inline unsigned int plBonusLib_8003F294_inline(plActionStats* stats, int attack)` returning `pl_800386D8(stats, attack)`.

Implemented `void fn_8003F294(int slot, int index)` using stale-move table access, action stats access, repeated calls to `pl_8003906C`, a magnitude calculation via `sqrtf__Ff`, and bit updates on `table->xDD1.bit5`.

The implementation uses recovered typed structures and fields where available, including `pl_StaleMoveTableExt_t`, `plActionStats`, `stats->hits.total`, `stats->attacks.total`, and stale move table fields such as `x0_staleMoveTable.xCDC` and `x0_staleMoveTable.xCE0`.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: declarations and field accesses in fn_8003F294

what_changed.text: The PR reports verification with `python configure.py --require-protos`, a targeted `plbonuslib.o` build, full `ninja`, and local objdiff showing `fn_8003F294` at 680 bytes and 100.0%.
evidence_refs: text_corpus issue_comment: bot report shows +680 bytes and `fn_8003F294` from 0.00% to 100.00%

smart_moves.text: Kept the global function name as address-style `fn_8003F294`, matching the PR's scoped target and avoiding unsupported semantic renaming.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: added `void fn_8003F294(int slot, int index)`

smart_moves.text: Included explicit verification details in the PR body and had a bot report corroborate the new match size and before/after status.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: verification commands and local objdiff

smart_moves.text: Used existing typed field names for several stats and stale-move-table accesses instead of representing all accesses as raw pointer arithmetic.

decomp_lessons.lesson: For a focused function-match PR, keep the scope narrow and pair the source replacement with a narrow verification ledger: require-protos, touched-object build, full build, and objdiff bytes/percentage for the matched function.
evidence_refs: text_corpus issue_comment: bot report for one new match

decomp_lessons.lesson: Address-style function names can remain appropriate when the PR evidence only proves a match target and does not prove semantic meaning.
A small wrapper inline may be used as a matching tactic when the PR's verified function match depends on a specific call expression shape, but this slice does not prove why the inline was required.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: `plBonusLib_8003F294_inline` wraps `pl_800386D8` and is used at multiple call sites

naming_conventions.name: fn_8003F294
naming_conventions.convention: Address-style function name retained for an unsemantically named matched target.

naming_conventions.name: plBonusLib_8003F294_inline
naming_conventions.convention: Helper inline name includes subsystem prefix and matched function address; evidence supports existence in this PR but not as a global naming standard.

naming_conventions.name: slot, index, table, stats
naming_conventions.convention: Locals use short role names where roles are evident from calls and types.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: `fn_8003F294(int slot, int index)`, `pl_StaleMoveTableExt_t* table`, `plActionStats* stats`

assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: The PR verifies the final match but does not explain why this stack padding was necessary.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used assignment expressions in call arguments, such as `threshold = pl_804D6470->x94`, likely to preserve evaluation/register shape.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: Supported as an observed tactic only; no review discussion explains intent.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used a do-while(0) wrapper around an early return on `index == 1`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: Observed source shape; the slice does not prove whether this was required for matching or stylistic.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Introduced a small inline wrapper around `pl_800386D8` and called the wrapper for attack stats lookups.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: Observed matching tactic; no explicit evidence explains codegen effect.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used typed field accesses for known structures and selective casts for unresolved or shape-sensitive accesses.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: Evidence supports mixed typed access and casts, not a broader recommendation to add casts generally.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.text: Bot report for GALE01 recorded matched code increasing by +680 bytes and listed one new match: `main/melee/pl/plbonuslib` item `fn_8003F294`, before 0.00%, after 100.00%.

follow_up_queries: Search later PRs for `plBonusLib_8003F294_inline` to see whether the wrapper remained or was cleaned up.
Search later PRs touching `fn_8003F294` or `plbonuslib.c` for renames, field recovery, or removal of `PAD_STACK(24)`.
Search for other `pl_8003906C` match PRs to compare whether assignment expressions in arguments are a repeated local matching pattern.
Search for `pl_StaleMoveTableExt_t` field naming updates around offsets `xD9C` through `xDD1`.

notes: There are no human review comments in the loaded slice, so reviewer intent and requested changes are not available.
The PR body and bot report support the match claim, but the slice does not include raw objdiff output beyond summarized percentages.

Several source shapes, including `PAD_STACK(24)`, the do-while(0), assignment expressions inside call arguments, and the inline wrapper, are observable tactics but their exact necessity is not explained in comments.
No evidence supports promoting semantic names for `fn_8003F294` or the x-offset fields beyond the names present in the diff.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Focused function matches should carry narrow verification evidence
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2565 is a compact example of a one-function match with PR-body verification commands and a bot report corroborating one new 680-byte 100.00% match.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: require-protos, targeted object build, full ninja, local objdiff

accepted_candidate_records.title: Retain address-style names when semantics are not proven
accepted_candidate_records.text: This PR implemented the target as `fn_8003F294` and did not invent a semantic global name; the loaded evidence only proves the address-style match target.

source_update_candidates.title: Document inline wrapper helpers as a narrow matching tactic requiring explanation
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2565 used a subsystem/address-named inline wrapper around `pl_800386D8` inside a verified 100.0% function match. Consider whether standards should mention that such wrappers are acceptable only with narrow match evidence and should be revisited if later cleanup can remove them.
source_update_candidates.reason: The tactic is observable and verified in this PR, but the slice does not prove general policy or why the inline was necessary.

source_update_candidates.title: Reinforce that PAD_STACK uses should be paired with match evidence and cleanup notes
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2565 includes `PAD_STACK(24)` in a verified function match, but no explanation of necessity. This is consistent with standards requiring explicit evidence for stack-padding tactics; curator may use it as an example if adding examples to the standard.
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful as an example of evidence-backed but unexplained stack padding; not enough to weaken the existing requirement for explicit tactic rationale.

rejection_notes: Do not promote `plBonusLib_8003F294_inline` as a general naming convention; this slice only shows one helper in one PR.
Do not infer semantic meanings for stale-move-table x-offset fields such as `xD9C`, `xDC8`, or `xDD1.bit5`; the diff preserves offset-style names.
Do not treat the do-while(0), assignment expressions in arguments, or casts as accepted style beyond this verified match without additional evidence.
Do not infer human reviewer approval details; no review comments were loaded.
